alt_forms.note: ⚠️ WH-specific: WH40K Japan uses 傷 (wounds) as its HP mechanic, tracking wounds taken rather than a health total. Avoid 傷 as an HP synonym in non-WH contexts.

definitions.gloss_en: a unit's total health points; the pool of vitality that is depleted by damage
definitions.gloss_jp: {ユニット|ゆにっと}の{HP|えいちぴー}；ダメージで{減|へ}っていく{生|せい}{命|めい}{力|りょく}の{総|そう}{量|りょう}
definitions.explanation_en: The numeric total of health a unit has. When depleted to zero, the unit is removed from play. The standard JP game term for HP across all genres.
definitions.implicit_notes: 体力 is the universal Japanese game term for HP, used for decades across all game genres. WH Japan uses 傷 (wounds) — a tracking-wounds-taken mechanic that should not be confused with a health-point total.
definitions.orthography_notes: 体力: 体 (body) + 力 (power/capacity). Weblio dictionary lists it as a general word. Used universally in Japanese games. WH Japan's 傷 (wound) mechanic is distinct and WH-specific.
definitions.game_setting_notes: Universal across all Japanese game genres. The docx explicitly notes: WH Japan uses 傷 (wounds) for HP — this is WH-specific and should be avoided. 体力 is the safe, universal term.

usage_jp: {体|たい}{力|りょく}が0になると{ユニット|ゆにっと}は{除|じょ}{去|きょ}される (when HP reaches 0, the unit is removed); {体|たい}{力|りょく}ゲージ (HP bar)
comparative_notes: 傷 (kizu, wound) is WH Japan's tracking mechanic and must be avoided in non-WH contexts. 体力 is the standard neutral Japanese HP term.

examples.japanese: {体|たい}{力|りょく}が0になったユニットは{戦|せん}{場|じょう}から{除|じょ}{去|きょ}される。
examples.english: Units whose HP reaches 0 are removed from the battlefield.

cross_references.note: 耐久力 — resilience/toughness (reduces damage taken, distinct from HP total)
